analysis.needed_for_promotion: no -- owned attention is promotable (default_eligible=true) without it
analysis.still_valuable: marginally -- full copy elimination on top of the owned route, but FO2 already removed the cast copy; the remaining materialization is paid by gqa too
analysis.blocked_by_owned_tile_correctness: no -- tile side fixed (dtype + ctx guard)
analysis.remaining_blocker: the opaque decode append writes NaN in-model (separate, still-open from docs/runtime-kv-graphrunner-arg-patch-result-20260623.md); GraphRunner arg patching was proven correct

recommendation: Defer. Owned attention + FO2 capture the bulk of the win byte-identically. Runtime-KV would only remove the residual materialization (incremental, also paid by gqa) and still has an open opaque-append-NaN bug. Resume only if a future audit shows the materialization is the dominant remaining decode tax; if resumed, scope from the post-fix state (do NOT reuse stale GraphRunner/cache-identity theories -- both refuted).
